content:
# John Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_001_page_001.jpg, JHN_001_page_002.jpg

1. In [the] beginning was the Logos, and the Logos was with the God, and God was the Logos.

2. This [one] was in [the] beginning with the God.

3. All things through him came into being, and apart from him came into being not even one [thing] that has come into being.

4. In him was life, and the life was the light of the men.

5. And the light in the darkness shines, and the darkness did not overcome it.

6. There came a man, having been sent from God, [the] name of him [was] Ioannes.

7. This [one] came for testimony, that he might testify concerning the light, that all might believe through him.

8. That [one] was not the light, but [came] that he might testify concerning the light.

9. The true light, which enlightens every man, was coming into the world.

10. In the world he was, and the world through him came into being, and the world did not know him.

11. To the own [things] he came, and the own [people] did not receive him.

12. But as many as received him, he gave to them authority to become children of God — to the [ones] believing in the name of him —

13. who not from bloods, nor from [the] will of flesh, nor from [the] will of a man, but from God were born.

14. And the Logos flesh became and tabernacled among us, and we beheld the glory of him, glory as of [the] only-begotten from [the] Father, full of grace and truth.

15. Ioannes testifies concerning him and has cried out, saying, "This was [the one] of whom I said, 'The [one] coming after me has come to be before me, for he was first of me.'"

16. For from the fullness of him we all received, and grace upon grace.

17. For the law through Mouses was given; the grace and the truth through Yeshua Christos came.

18. God no one has ever seen. The only-begotten God, the [one] being in the bosom of the Father — that [one] made [him] known.

19. And this is the testimony of Ioannes, when the Ioudaioi sent to him priests and Levites from Hierosolyma that they might ask him, "Who are you?"

20. And he confessed and did not deny, and confessed, "I am not the Christos."

21. And they asked him, "What then? Are you Elias?" And he says, "I am not." "Are you the Prophet?" And he answered, "No."

22. Then they said to him, "Who are you? — that we may give an answer to the [ones] having sent us. What do you say concerning yourself?"

23. He said, "I [am the] voice of [one] crying in the wilderness, 'Make straight the way of [the] Lord,' just as Esaias the prophet said."

24. And [some] had been sent from the Pharisaioi.

25. And they asked him and said to him, "Why then do you baptize, if you are not the Christos, nor Elias, nor the Prophet?"

26. Ioannes answered them, saying, "I baptize in water. In [the] midst of you stands [one] whom you do not know,

27. the [one] coming after me, of whom I am not worthy that I should loose the strap of the sandal of him."

28. These things happened in Bethania beyond the Iordanes, where Ioannes was baptizing.

29. On the morrow he sees the Yeshua coming to him and says, "Behold, the Lamb of the God, the [one] taking away the sin of the world.

30. This is [the one] concerning whom I said, 'After me comes a man who has come to be before me, for he was first of me.'

31. And I did not know him, but that he might be revealed to Israel — because of this I came baptizing in water."

32. And Ioannes testified, saying, "I have beheld the Spirit descending as a dove out of heaven, and it remained upon him.

33. And I did not know him, but the [one] having sent me to baptize in water, that [one] said to me, 'Upon whom you see the Spirit descending and remaining upon him, this is the [one] baptizing in [the] Holy Spirit.'

34. And I have seen and have testified that this is the Son of the God."

35. On the morrow again Ioannes was standing and [two] of the disciples of him,

36. and having looked at the Yeshua walking, he says, "Behold, the Lamb of the God."

37. And the two disciples heard him speaking, and they followed the Yeshua.

38. And the Yeshua, having turned and having beheld them following, says to them, "What do you seek?" And they said to him, "Rabbi" — which is said, being translated, Teacher — "where do you stay?"

39. He says to them, "Come and you will see." They came therefore and saw where he stays, and they stayed with him that day. The hour was about the tenth.

40. Andreas, the brother of Simon Petros, was one of the two having heard from Ioannes and having followed him.

41. This [one] finds first the own brother Simon and says to him, "We have found the Messias" — which is, being translated, Christos.

42. He led him to the Yeshua. Having looked at him, the Yeshua said, "You are Simon the son of Ioannes; you will be called Kephas" — which is translated Petros.

43. On the morrow he wished to go out to the Galilaia, and he finds Philippos. And the Yeshua says to him, "Follow me."

44. And Philippos was from Bethsaida, from the city of Andreas and Petros.

45. Philippos finds Nathanael and says to him, "The [one] of whom Mouses in the law and the prophets wrote, we have found — Yeshua, the son of Ioseph, the [one] from Nazaret."

46. And Nathanael said to him, "From Nazaret can anything good be?" Philippos says to him, "Come and see."

47. The Yeshua saw Nathanael coming to him and says concerning him, "Behold, truly an Israelite in whom there is no deceit."

48. Nathanael says to him, "From where do you know me?" The Yeshua answered and said to him, "Before Philippos called you, being under the fig tree, I saw you."

49. Nathanael answered him, "Rabbi, you are the Son of the God! You are the King of Israel!"

50. The Yeshua answered and said to him, "Because I said to you that I saw you under the fig tree, you believe? Greater [things] than these you will see."

51. And he says to him, "Truly, truly I say to you, you will see the heaven having been opened, and the angels of the God ascending and descending upon the Son of the Man."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "God was the Logos":** The Greek word order is θεὸς ἦν ὁ λόγος — "God was the Word." The predicate nominative (θεός, without the article) precedes the verb, emphasizing the divine nature of the Logos while distinguishing the Logos from ὁ θεός ("the God" = the Father). EMET preserves the Greek word order.

2. **v.13 — "bloods" (plural):** ἐξ αἱμάτων — the unusual plural "bloods" likely refers to bloodlines or physical descent. The entire phrase denies that divine birth comes through human lineage, sexual desire, or male initiative.

3. **v.14 — "tabernacled":** ἐσκήνωσεν — from σκηνή ("tent/tabernacle"). The Logos "pitched his tent" among us, echoing the tabernacle (σκηνή) in the wilderness where God's glory dwelt (Exodus 40:34-35). EMET preserves this allusion rather than flattening it to "dwelt."

4. **v.18 — "The only-begotten God":** Sinaiticus reads μονογενὴς θεός ("only-begotten God"), not μονογενὴς υἱός ("only-begotten Son") as in many later manuscripts. This is one of the most significant textual variants in the entire NT. The Sinaiticus/Alexandrian reading is bolder: Jesus is not merely the unique Son but the unique God who reveals the Father.

5. **v.28 — "Bethania beyond the Jordan":** Not the Bethany near Jerusalem (where Lazarus lived). This is a different location east of the Jordan, associated with John's baptismal ministry. Some manuscripts read "Bethabara" (a correction by Origen), but Sinaiticus preserves "Bethania."

6. **v.34 — "the Son of the God":** Sinaiticus reads ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ ("the Son of God"). Some important manuscripts (notably P5, P106) read ὁ ἐκλεκτὸς τοῦ θεοῦ ("the Chosen One of God"). The Sinaiticus reading is christologically explicit.

7. **v.41 — "Messias":** Μεσσίας — one of only two uses of this Hebrew/Aramaic title in the NT (the other is John 4:25). John carefully translates it for Greek readers: "which is, being translated, Christ."

— EMET, AI Translator
